action.reasoning: Observation: Best bal_acc=0.6196 (iter21: s1=60ep with mixup/cutmix p=0.5 rand choice + Focal gamma2 + sqrt-sampler + AdamW5e-4 cosine + RandAug, s2=40ep CE+count-sampler + SGD0.05 mom0.9 nesterov on fc-only after freeze, post-train tau~1.45 grid val=0.608). iter22 (with AutoAug+ s1=80) slightly worse at 0.6181 (tau=1.15 val_tau=0.639). Current train.py has AutoAugment (over-aug per analysis), incomplete logs in some places, truncated display but functional end. Model/predict stable (RN50 CIFAR-adapted, weights_only=False, best_tau+counts in ckpt, batched norm+logit adj). Iter time ~7min, 1.46h/7 iters left. Analysis: cRT+Focal+mix/cut rand+RandAug+post-tau strong for CIFAR100-LT IR=100; removing AutoAugment + exact s1=60/s2=40 should recover/reach >=0.6196; explicit log_metrics after EVERY val (both stages) + per-20step loss ensures full curves without changing core logic. No need for new backbone yet (capacity good, variance from seeds/aug). Decision: Fully rewrite clean train.py (incremental from current structure: drop AutoAugment, set 60/40 eps, add mix/cut rand p=0.5 in s1 loop, call log_metrics after each val + periodic loss, proper named_param freeze after s1 best, best-save across stages+post-tau grid 0-3@0.05, seeds, metrics only, complete loops/CKPT/validate). Run training then submit_eval immediately (targets >=0.62, follows >20ep rule with immediate eval, fits budget).
